desc: Rome is a year-round capital of antiquity and street life where blockbuster monuments coexist with lived-in neighborhoods, market streets, and late-night piazza culture. The city is at its most comfortable in spring and early autumn, while high summer brings intense heat and slower local rhythms, and winter offers shorter queues at major museums.
hls: Ancient Rome icons: Colosseum, Forum, Pantheon
Vatican Museums and St Peter's Basilica
Layered neighborhoods from Centro Storico to Trastevere
Deep museum bench (Borghese, Capitoline, Baths of Caracalla)
Strong food scene from trattorie to specialty coffee
Excellent rail hub for Florence, Naples, and beyond

todo.name: Colosseum, Forum & Palatine at opening time
todo.desc: Book an early slot and pair the three sites in one morning before midday heat and tour-group peaks.

todo.name: Vatican Museums + St Peter's combo day
todo.desc: Use prebooked timed entry, then cross to the basilica and dome for city panoramas.

todo.name: Pantheon, Piazza Navona, Campo de' Fiori loop
todo.desc: A compact walking circuit linking Rome's baroque core, fountains, and café stops.

todo.name: Borghese Gallery reservation slot
todo.desc: Secure tickets well ahead for Bernini and Caravaggio highlights in a manageable two-hour visit.

todo.name: Appian Way cycle + Catacombs
todo.desc: Half-day route that combines Roman road engineering with early-Christian underground sites.

todo.name: Testaccio food evening
todo.desc: Focus on Roman classics in a local district less polished than central tourist corridors.

todo.name: Baths of Caracalla and Circus Maximus corridor
todo.desc: Explore monumental ruins with more space than the Forum area.

todo.name: Tivoli day trip (Villa d'Este or Hadrian's Villa)
todo.desc: Easy rail/bus escape for Renaissance gardens or imperial archaeology outside city crowds.

wifi.note: Apartments and business hotels are usually reliable; 5G is strong across central districts. Ask hosts for router placement in older palazzo buildings where interior walls can reduce room-to-room speeds.

prac.fltNote: Direct flights are available from Gatwick; London City typically requires a connection. Typical return fares are often ~£45–£110 in deep off-peak, ~£110–£190 in shoulder months, and ~£190+ around Easter, summer school holidays, and late-December peaks.
prac.visa: Italy is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can typically visit visa-free for up to 90 days in any rolling 180-day period.
alerts: Major sites now rely on timed-entry inventory — prebook Colosseum/Vatican/Borghese slots before arrival.
July–August heat can exceed comfort for midday walking on exposed archaeology sites; start early and plan indoor breaks.
Around Easter, long Italian weekends, and Christmas/New Year, central-room rates and flight prices rise quickly.

sweet: Mid-April to June and mid-September to late October balance warm weather, long sightseeing windows, and fewer heat-related disruptions than peak summer.
